hypothesis: A Transformer-based patch-embedded encoder-decoder model pretrained on simulated IR spectra and fine-tuned on experimental spectra, jointly trained with a reconstruction network that aligns encoder representations to pure-component spectra, can accurately identify the molecular constituents of binary and ternary IR mixture spectra (including imbalanced mixtures and real ATR-IR measurements) as measured by high Top-1/5/10 accuracy and Tanimoto similarity.
